name: Restore Repository-Root Railway Deployment

root_causes.description: railpack.json's custom steps.install.commands override replaced Railpack's auto-generated install step, losing whatever implicit inputs wiring gives that step access to the copied repository files; the install step ran against an empty working directory.
Railway's startCommand/preDeployCommand parser rejects bash control-flow syntax (if/then/fi) outright -- discovered and reverted within this same investigation.

A root-level pyproject.toml/uv.lock pair (belonging to tools/deployment_observer/'s own dev tooling) sat at the same directory level as backend/'s own dependency files once rootDirectory widened to the repo root, causing Railpack to intermittently select uv and the wrong project's (empty) dependency set instead of pip and backend/'s real dependencies.

Railpack's pip-mode install target does not appear to be on the runtime container's sys.path -- USER_SITE ('/root/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages') confirmed not to exist in the deployed image via a live 'python -m site' diagnostic, resulting in 'No module named alembic' despite alembic being correctly declared as a dependency and the build succeeding.

mitigations_attempted_for_root_cause_4.description: Prepend /app/.venv/bin to PATH in start/pre-deploy commands
mitigations_attempted_for_root_cause_4.result: no effect -- no python binary exists at that path for this build

mitigations_attempted_for_root_cause_4.description: Set PIP_USER=0 as a Railway service environment variable
mitigations_attempted_for_root_cause_4.result: no effect -- Railway does not inject regular service variables into the build step

diagnostic_findings.preDeployCommand_stdout_capture: unreliable via Railway's deploy-log stream regardless of command content, confirmed via Railway's own support agent exhaustively searching every available log filter

regression_results.ci_status: green on every PR that triggered CI (path-filtered workflows did not trigger for config-only or deployment_observer-only changes; verified locally in those cases)

live_service_health_during_investigation: 200 {"status": "ok"} confirmed repeatedly before, during, and after every deploy attempt -- never degraded

final_deployment_status.runtime_status: fails at container startup, No module named alembic

recommendations: Open a Railway support thread referencing service 43195c7a-8c18-4711-98a8-633d280a3b77 and deployment 96d1f190-f595-4ede-b8a5-c67209466e3c to inspect the actual pip install output from the Metal builder
Attempt a custom railpack.json install step with explicit --no-user/--target flags and correct inputs wiring, now that this investigation understands why the earlier custom-install-step attempt broke
The previous /backend-scoped deployment continues to serve production safely with no urgency from an availability perspective
